findings.evidence: Grep hits: line 61 has `variable {M : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [StarAddMonoid M]`; line 164 has `[AddCommGroup N]`; Mathlib defines `class StarAddMonoid (R) [AddMonoid R]` at `Mathlib/Algebra/Star/Basic.lean:232`, and the file’s uses are `v i + star (v j)`, `a + star b`, and `star_zero`, not domain commutativity.
findings.fix: Weaken the domain assumptions to `[AddMonoid M] [StarAddMonoid M]`, and similarly use `[AddGroup N] [StarAddMonoid N]` in the group section unless a theorem genuinely needs commutativity.
findings.issue: `IsPositiveDefinite` and its basic API are globally restricted to `[AddCommMonoid M]`, and the group bound repeats this with `[AddCommGroup N]`, but the statements only form `x + star y` and never commute elements of the domain.

summary: The predicate and API are at the intended level except for an unnecessary commutativity requirement on the domain typeclasses.
